records.ety: [F. fortune, L. fortuna; akin to fors, fortis, chance, prob. fr. ferre to bear, bring. See Bear to support, and cf. Fortuitous.]

defs: The arrival of something in a sudden or unexpected manner; chance; accident; luck; hap; also, the personified or deified power regarded as determining human success, apportioning happiness and unhappiness, and distributing arbitrarily or fortuitously the lots of life.
quotes: 'T is more by fortune, lady, than by merit.
O Fortune, Fortune, all men call thee fickle.

defs: That which befalls or is to befall one; lot in life, or event in any particular undertaking; fate; destiny; as, to tell one's fortune.
quotes: You, who men's fortunes in their faces read.

defs: That which comes as the result of an undertaking or of a course of action; good or ill success; especially, favorable issue; happy event; success; prosperity as reached partly by chance and partly by effort.
quotes: Our equal crimes shall equal fortune give.
There is a tide in the affairs of men, Which, taken at the flood, leads on to fortune.
His father dying, he was driven to seek his fortune.

defs: Wealth; large possessions; large estate; riches; as, a gentleman of fortune.

records.ety: [OF. fortuner, L. fortunare. See Fortune, n.]

defs: To make fortunate; to give either good or bad fortune to.
To presage; to tell the fortune of.

quotes: It fortuned the same night that a Christian, serving a Turk in the camp, secretely gave the watchmen warning.
